5.text: Hmmm my thoughts on this - i don't think that the 2.4mio Filipinos actually "wait" in the middle east. There is very little who requested for repatriation as you also mentioned. All my relatives and friends who are in UAE for example do not wish to be sent back. Also, its not the President job to send them back home - it is the DFA's job isnt it? His job is to handle diplomatic engagement, not to operationally oversee evacuation logistics. Also, i think he is going to the US not to engage with the US politics but to the UN, which just happened to be there and is very normal that meetings like this happen whenever there is an international crisis. I actually think that it is good that he is going to this UN meeting because the broader geopolitical reality is that countries like the Philippines often use the UN because we lack direct leverage in middle east conflicts, so diplomacy through multilateral institutions is one of the few tools available.
I think magpapamedical yan sya... Cover lang yan

HI MCT,

Good day po.

May i offer some coffee thoughts on this: "The Philippines is in the middle of the most severe global oil disruption to affect its economy in years, driven in part by a war launched by the country Marcos is visiting. No bilateral with Trump is on the schedule despite the Philippines being one of the most directly affected non-combatant nations in the conflict."

To my perception, Marcos is visiting the United Nations, not the US.  It seems the sentence that Marcos is visiting the country that launched a war implies that Marcos is visiting the US. 

The thing is, the United Nations's HQ happens to be in the US and we all know that it is headquartered in New York City because an $8.5 million donation by John D. Rockefeller Jr. allowed for the purchase of a permanent site along the East River in 1946.  

In this context, I am inclined to perceive it as Marcos visiting the United Nations, not the US. Thus, Escalona called it "the most suitable platform" ; it is the platform for all member nations in the UN. Iran is a member of the UN; and so is Israel, and the Arab gulf countries - the United Nations is 193 nations.  The United Nations is not the US.  Marcos will thus be addressing 193 countries and two recognized sovereign states that hold non-member observer status -- Vatican City (the Holy See) and Palestine.   This is a Philippine line of effort in multilateral diplomacy.

That Marcos will also address the  UN's Commission on the Status of Women is an interesting coincidence with the Suntay issue.

Another interesting piece of news -- "According to reports, permanent membership in the "Board of Peace" requires a payment of US$1 billion. This initiative, led by the Trump administration for Gaza reconstruction, has invited dozens of world leaders to join, with the fee required to extend membership beyond three years".

May i refer to these lines --- "There is one outstanding diplomatic item hanging over the trip.  Trump recently invited the Philippines to join his Board of Peace, a US-led initiative focused on the reconstruction of Gaza. Philippine Ambassador to the US Jose Manuel Romualdez confirmed that Trump sent a personal letter to Marcos, but Manila is still evaluating the nature of the commitment required".

"Escalona declined to elaborate on the status of that proposal".

--- coffee thoughts on this:  the Philippines does not have US$1B to pay as "membership fees" to be part of the Board of Peace.  Although the invitation is there, the question is: does it mean membership is free?  

Recalling: As of mid-2025, President Donald Trump has demanded that NATO allies significantly increase their defense spending to 5% of their GDP, up from the current 2% target, pushing for European nations to fund more of their own security. This move presses for a massive increase in European contributions, which, if not met, may lead to a reduced U.S. role in European defense. Trump has pushed for NATO allies to increase defense investment to 5% of their gross domestic product (GDP). This demand came as European nations, led by Germany and France, have already increased their spending, with Germany raising its expenditure by 24% to $93.7 billion in 2024.  The demand signals a potentially major restructuring of NATO, with the US potentially reducing its role in European defense, which may require European nations to significantly bolster their own capabilities over the next 5–10 years, says the European Union Institute for Security Studies.  Despite the increased spending in 2024, the demand for 5% represents a dramatic shift from the long-standing 2% goal, making it a central point of tension in NATO.

PS: On January 8, 2026, President Donald Trump announced the U.S. would withdraw from 66 UN and international organizations, including the UNFCCC climate treaty. While not a full withdrawal from the United Nations itself, this move targeted major UN entities deemed contrary to U.S. interests. 

Sources:

https://www.un.org/sites/un2.un.org/files/headquarters.pdf 

https://www.whitehouse.gov/fact-sheets/2026/01/fact-sheet-president-donald-j-trump-withdraws-the-united-states-from-international-organizations-that-are-contrary-to-the-interests-of-the-united-states/ 

https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2026/1/8/trump-to-withdraw-us-from-dozens-of-un-international-organisations

https://news.sky.com/story/us-wants-countries-to-pay-1bn-to-stay-on-gaza-peace-board-13495801 

https://www.iss.europa.eu/publications/briefs/fit-purpose-reforming-nato-age-trump-20

https://www.gov.uk/government/publications/international-defence-expenditure-2025/finance-and-economics-annual-statistical-bulletin-international-defence-2025

https://www.politico.eu/article/donald-trump-5-percent-defense-spending-target-far-fetched-nato/

That one-day work from home week for the judiciary means the hearings will be conducted online via videoconference instead of in person, with certain exceptions. For party-litigants, if they cannot appear via vidcon, their lawyers can ask them to execute an SPA ahead of time so they won’t have to attend online. In civil cases, litigants are only required to appear in person before the court for the pre-trial. The rest, they can ask their counsel to represent them thru an SPA. Even in criminal cases, the accused is only required to appear during the arraignment and the promulgation of the decision. They can execute a waiver of appearance for the duration of the trial.

What’s often overlooked is that the U.S. today is far less dependent on Middle Eastern oil than it used to be. Many Asian and some European economies rely on it much more. Also, most foreign workers in the region aren’t Americans but migrants from countries like India, the Philippines, and Pakistan—so we are often the ones most directly exposed when tensions escalate. Global oil prices affect everyone, but in terms of direct dependence and people on the ground, Asia and parts of Europe tend to feel the impact more.

17.text:
The biggest problem here is that most agencies--like MMDA--will implement it in a different way.

If an agency opts for a compressed work week (4 days work, 10 hours of per day), isn't that going to result in the same amount of energy consumed as a 5-day, 8-hour work week?

If the objective of the 4 day workweek is to reduce the transportation expenses of employees. I believe such objective  can be attained.

However, by stretching the working hours, productivity is affected. A human comprehension is effective only on the 1st 45 minutes. It plateaus for the rest of the day. In an 8 hour workday, an 80% daily productivity is the ideal number. But hardly consistently achievable. What more of a 10 hour workday.

Productivity has never been about long hours. 

Why not retain a 4 day 8 hour workday and a work from home on the 5th?

On this International Women’s Day, which also falls on the Third Sunday of Lent, we lift our prayers through the loving intercession of Mary, Mother of Jesus. 🙏🌸

Lord, as Jesus Christ showed compassion and dignity to the Samaritan Woman at the Well, teach us to recognize and honor the worth of every woman.

All women deserve to be celebrated and respected. No explanation or justification should be required. It is simply a matter of basic human decency—to honor the strength, dignity, and contributions of women everywhere. ✨

May all women be strengthened in faith, hope, and love, and may their lives continue to reflect God’s grace and compassion in the world. 💙🙏
